page: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is a memory-optimized server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 48 virtual cores under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across compute and memory subsystems. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance registers in the top 10% tier at 3702.98, while multi-core performance reaches 88688.86, placing it in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with large-block read-only and write speeds reaching 414.9 GB/sec and 213.09 GB/sec respectively, both scoring in the top 10% tier. Database operations via Passmark score 30834.1, and Redis SET operations achieve 85.37 million operations per second.

A primary architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained, concurrent multi-core workloads compared to dedicated instances. However, the high resource density of 384.0 GB of RAM and 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides significant qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated 100% CPU utilization.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database hosting, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks where high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage are critical.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 48 shared vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds at 414.9 GB/sec and write speeds at 213.09 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core CPU performance. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained compute-heavy tasks, the high memory-to-core ratio and substantial NVMe storage offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-dense workloads. This instance is highly suited for large databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2880 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring forty-eight shared cores, substantial NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
2880 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
